new_classes.label_en: Other Vinegar (not Wine Vinegar) in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22090099)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Essig (kein Weinessig) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22090099)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, other than wine vinegar, presented in containers of more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2209 00 99 (Chapter 22). Includes, for example, malt vinegar, cider vinegar, spirit vinegar, and other non-wine-based vinegars as described in the HS Explanatory Notes to heading 2209.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 22090099 are: (1) it is a vinegar or substitute for vinegar obtained from acetic acid; (2) it is NOT wine vinegar (i.e. other than wine vinegar, as 22090091 covers small containers ≤2L and 22090099 covers large containers >2L); (3) it is presented in containers of MORE than 2 litres. These criteria are captured by the subclass relationship to eucn:Vinegar, the codeString hasValue, and the minExclusive container volume restriction. The complement restriction on WineVinegar is included to signal exclusion of wine vinegar. Coverage is not 1.0 because: the legal note references HS Explanatory Notes Part I (2nd paragraph, items 2, 3, 4) and Part II describing specific product types (e.g. malt vinegar, spirit vinegar, cider vinegar), which are mentioned in the source note but not individually axiomatised here as subclasses. Additionally, the complement restriction as modelled uses an object-property/class value approach which is a simplification. The sibling disjointness with OtherVinegarSmallContainer (22090091) and WineVinegar classes is implied but not explicitly asserted as an OWL disjointWith axiom in the restrictions array.
